title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description:
Given a string `s`, return the **longest palindromic substring** in `s`.

A palindrome is a string that reads the same forwards and backwards. If there are multiple answers of the same length, return any one of them.
constraints: s consists of only digits and English letters.
solution:
# Problem: Find the longest substring of s that is a palindrome.
#
# Approach: "Expand around center" technique. Every palindrome has a center.
# For each possible center (single char or between two chars), expand outward
# while characters match. Track the longest palindrome found.
#
# Time complexity: O(n^2) — up to 2n centers, each expansion is O(n).
# Space complexity: O(1) — only tracking start index and max length.
#
# Key steps:
# 1. Define a helper that expands from a center and returns the palindrome bounds.
# 2. Iterate over each index, expanding for both odd-length and even-length cases.
# 3. Update the best result whenever a longer palindrome is found.

def longest_palindrome(s: str) -> str:
    if len(s) < 2:
        return s

    start = 0
    max_len = 1

    def expand(left: int, right: int) -> tuple[int, int]:
        while left >= 0 and right < len(s) and s[left] == s[right]:
            left -= 1
            right += 1
        # left+1 and right-1 are the actual palindrome bounds
        return left + 1, right - 1

    for i in range(len(s)):
        # Odd-length palindrome centered at i
        lo, hi = expand(i, i)
        if hi - lo + 1 > max_len:
            start = lo
            max_len = hi - lo + 1

        # Even-length palindrome centered between i and i+1
        lo, hi = expand(i, i + 1)
        if hi - lo + 1 > max_len:
            start = lo
            max_len = hi - lo + 1

    return s[start:start + max_len]
